purpose: Clarify that project creation and project evolution should still use LLMs when useful. The fix is not to avoid LLMs, but to make LLM output robustly normalizable from multiple formats into safe proposal/artifact types.
core_clarification: LLM-assisted planning/propose is desired. The problem is only raw, unvalidated, strict-chat-JSON dependency. A well-prepared LLM may return tool_calls, JSON, Markdown, patches, file blocks, shell commands, unified diffs or a natural-language plan. The Hub must normalize/repair/classify these into ExecutableProposal, PatchProposal, FileProposal, PlannerProposalArtifact or needs_review.
principles_to_merge: LLM should be used for flexible project creation and project evolution when deterministic templates are insufficient.
Deterministic handlers are fast-paths and guardrails, not a replacement for LLM reasoning.
The Hub should prepare LLM calls with rich context, tool schemas, output schema, expected artifacts, examples and constraints.
LLM responses may arrive in different formats; the Hub needs a normalization pipeline instead of one brittle parser.
Only normalized ExecutableProposal with command/tool_calls can execute directly.
Patch/file/Markdown/natural-language outputs can be converted into proposal artifacts or repair-normalized proposals, but not executed raw.
Failed normalization should move to next strategy, retry once with repair prompt, or needs_review; never infinite retry.

new_strategy_layer.name: LLMResponseNormalizationPipeline
new_strategy_layer.position: inside ToolCallingLLMStrategy, JsonSchemaLLMStrategy, WorkerStrategy and LegacySgptStrategy result handling

tasks_to_merge.title: Add LLMResponseNormalizer service
tasks_to_merge.goal: Normalize heterogeneous LLM/worker outputs into safe proposal/result types instead of relying on one strict JSON parser.
acceptance_criteria: Normalizer accepts raw_text, provider_response, tool_calls, task_context and expected_output_contract.
OpenAI-compatible tool_calls normalize directly into ExecutableProposal.tool_calls.
Strict JSON matching ExecutableProposal schema normalizes into ExecutableProposal.
JSON inside Markdown code fences is extracted and schema-validated.
Shell command code block may normalize into ExecutableProposal.command only when command policy and task policy allow shell commands.
Unified diff normalizes into PatchProposalArtifact, not directly executed unless apply_patch strategy approves it.
File blocks with paths normalize into FileProposalArtifact or tool_calls when write_file tool is available and paths are safe.
Plain natural-language plan normalizes into AdvisoryProposalArtifact, not ExecutableProposal.
Every normalization result includes confidence, source_format, reason_codes and safety_warnings.
test_requirements: Tests for tool_calls, strict JSON, fenced JSON, shell block, unified diff, file blocks and plain natural language.
anti_patterns: Do not treat a natural-language plan as a command.
Do not extract shell commands unless policy allows shell execution.

tasks_to_merge.title: Add LLMRepairAndCoercionStrategy for near-valid outputs
tasks_to_merge.goal: When an LLM response is close to usable, repair/coerce once into the expected proposal type instead of failing immediately or looping forever.
acceptance_criteria: Repair strategy runs at most once per strategy attempt unless policy explicitly allows more.
Repair prompt includes original response, validation errors, target schema and allowed output formats.
Repair result must still pass ExecutableProposal/Patch/FileProposal schema validation.
If repair fails, result becomes advisory or needs_review according to policy, not retry-loop.
Repair never broadens permissions or bypasses allowed_tools/context policy.
Repair telemetry records original_format, repair_attempted, repair_success and reason_codes.
test_requirements: Tests for malformed JSON repaired, Markdown JSON repaired, unrecoverable prose becomes advisory, max one repair attempt.
anti_patterns: Do not repair unsafe shell into executable command without policy.

tasks_to_merge.title: Prepare LLM calls with rich project/task context and expected outputs
tasks_to_merge.goal: Use LLMs well: provide task goal, existing project context, allowed tools, expected artifacts, constraints and examples so the model can produce useful flexible output.
acceptance_criteria: LLM prompt/context includes goal, task title, task_kind, workspace summary, relevant CodeCompass/RAG context if allowed, allowed tools, expected artifacts and output contract.
Context is filtered by ContextAccessPolicy before being sent to local/remote/cloud model.
ToolCallingLLMStrategy passes real tool schemas where supported.
JsonSchemaLLMStrategy passes explicit response schema where supported.
If provider lacks tools/schema support, strategy returns declined or uses configured flexible-normalization mode, not hidden brittle parsing.
Tests verify sensitive context is not included for cloud destinations and expected artifacts are included.
test_requirements: Context preparation tests for local model, cloud model, and project creation task.
anti_patterns: Do not send full project/secrets to cloud by default.
Do not call LLM with vague prompt and no output contract.

tasks_to_merge.title: Make strategy policy support LLM-assisted modes per task kind
tasks_to_merge.goal: Allow users to configure when the LLM is primary, assistant, fallback or disabled per task kind.
acceptance_criteria: ProposePolicy supports llm_mode values disabled, fallback, assisted, primary_with_guardrails.
new_software_project can be configured as primary_with_guardrails or assisted, so LLM can design flexible project structure while output is normalized safely.
coding can prefer deterministic_handler first but still use LLM-assisted fallback for non-template changes.
repair can require deterministic repair runner before LLM-assisted proposal.
Policy supports accepted_output_formats list, e.g. tool_calls, executable_json, fenced_json, unified_diff, file_blocks, command_block, advisory_text.
Policy supports normalize_then_execute boolean and require_human_review_for_shell boolean.
Unsafe combination allow_unstructured_text_as_execution=true remains rejected by default.
test_requirements: Policy validation tests for assisted, primary_with_guardrails, disabled and unsafe config rejection.
anti_patterns: Do not make LLM usage all-or-nothing.
Do not allow unstructured text to execute directly.

tasks_to_merge.title: Add flexible-output E2E tests for project creation and evolution
tasks_to_merge.goal: Prove that useful LLM outputs in several formats can be normalized safely into proposals/artifacts and completed through artifact-first verification.
acceptance_criteria: Project creation test accepts mocked LLM tool_calls and executes them.
Project creation test accepts mocked fenced JSON and normalizes it.
Project creation test accepts mocked file blocks and converts them to write_file tool_calls or FileProposalArtifact.
Project evolution test accepts mocked unified diff and stores PatchProposalArtifact before apply/approval.
Plain natural-language output becomes AdvisoryProposalArtifact and does not execute.
All executable paths still produce ArtifactManifest/FileChangeSet and pass verification before task completion.
test_requirements: Mocked LLM responses only; no live Ollama/LM Studio dependency required.
anti_patterns: Do not write tests that only cover perfect JSON.
Do not skip artifact verification after flexible normalization.

definition_of_done_extension: LLM use is explicitly supported and configurable for project creation and project evolution.
LLM outputs in tool_calls, JSON, fenced JSON, diff, file blocks, command blocks and natural language are handled safely.
Only normalized executable outputs can run.
Non-executable LLM outputs become proposal/advisory artifacts or human review, not errors or retry loops.
Artifact-first completion remains mandatory after execution.
